prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for pyridine (11 atoms: N + 5C + 5H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF backend, chemkit fukui-reactivity skill), gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1 (neutral); cation: charge +1 / mult 2; anion: charge −1 / mult 2. Geometry taken directly from the user-supplied XYZ file (not re-optimized). Partial charges obtained via Mulliken scheme (PySCF). Fukui indices computed by finite difference of Mulliken charges across the N, N−1, N+1 electron states.

Headline result: the most electrophilic site is N (atom 1) with f⁺ = 0.48422572. The most nucleophilic site is C (atom 4) with f⁻ = 0.11826260.
